summary: US CENTCOM conducted strikes against Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar facilities on June 26 in response to Iran's attack on the Singapore-flagged cargo vessel M/V Ever Lovely in the Strait of Hormuz on June 25. Israel and Lebanon signed a US-brokered framework agreement aimed at dismantling Hezbollah's armed presence and establishing Lebanese state authority, triggering popular protests in Beirut. Iranian IRGC naval forces reportedly attacked positions belonging to US forces in response to the American strikes on southern Iran.
key_points: US CENTCOM struck Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar facilities on June 26, citing Iran's June 25 attack on M/V Ever Lovely in the Strait of Hormuz as justification. (Source: @shin_persian/7223)
Iran's IRGC naval forces attacked several positions belonging to US forces in response to American strikes on southern Iran, according to Iranian state media. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373061)

Iranian authorities stated IRGC returned three foreign tankers attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz via a southern corridor near Oman, declaring the alternative route illegal and dangerous. (Source: @VahidOnline/76691)

Israel and Lebanon signed a US-brokered framework agreement requiring Hezbollah's disarmament, with Israel maintaining its security zone along the Yellow Line until armed groups are dismantled. (Source: @inon_yttach/9176)

Popular protests erupted in Beirut around the government palace (Saray) and airport road following the Lebanon-Israel agreement signing, with Lebanese Army deploying tear gas against demonstrators. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80014)

IDF Spokesperson reported seven Hezbollah militants were eliminated while transferring weapons in a structure at al-Manzala in southern Lebanon, in an operation directed by Unit Egoz. (Source: @amitsegal/62556)

Israeli Defense Minister Katz warned in Persian that if Iran attacks Israel it would be its greatest mistake, stating nothing would stop Israeli forces from completing their mission. (Source: @amitsegal/62555)
22 Iranian tanker crew members previously seized by the US were handed over to Islamic Republic authorities in Karachi, Pakistan. (Source: @shin_persian/7212)

sections.executive_summary: US CENTCOM conducted retaliatory strikes against Iranian military infrastructure on June 26 following Iran's drone attack on a Singapore-flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, while Iran's IRGC responded by attacking US force positions. Simultaneously, Israel and Lebanon signed a US-brokered framework agreement requiring Hezbollah's disarmament and extension of Lebanese state authority, triggering widespread protests in Beirut. These parallel developments indicate a significant escalation in both the Gulf maritime security front and the Lebanon-Israel diplomatic-military track.

points: IRGC naval forces targeted several positions belonging to US forces in response to American strikes on southern Iran, according to Iranian state broadcaster IRIB. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373061)

IRGC returned three foreign tankers attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz via a southern corridor near Oman, declaring the alternative route proposed by Oman and IMO as illegal and dangerous. (Source: @VahidOnline/76691)
Iranian state media reported an explosion was heard in Taheruya, Sirik, in southern Iran, with the source of the sound not immediately known. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4411)
22 Iranian tanker crew members previously seized by US forces were handed over to Islamic Republic authorities in Karachi, Pakistan. (Source: @shin_persian/7212)
Iranian IRGC stated it targeted several US force positions in retaliation for the American strikes conducted against Iranian territory. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93262)

IDF eliminated seven Hezbollah militants at al-Manzala in southern Lebanon who were transferring weapons into a structure serving as a firing and observation position against IDF forces, in an operation directed by Unit Egoz. (Source: @amitsegal/62556)

Israel and Lebanon signed a US-brokered framework agreement; Israel insisted on maintaining its security zone along the Yellow Line until Hezbollah and terrorist organizations are disarmed. (Source: @almogboker78/17596)

Israel rejected more than 10 Lebanese-American proposals for defining pilot zones for security arrangements, with the central dispute being Israel's demand for active Lebanese Army presence and enforcement north and south of the Litani River. (Source: @yediotnews25/131957)
Defense Minister Katz warned in Persian that an Iranian attack on Israel would be Tehran's greatest mistake, stating nothing including Hormuz would stop Israeli forces. (Source: @amitsegal/62555)
IDF reported over 10 eliminations in Gaza and 6 strikes in southern Lebanon during the week, along with over 100 arrests in the West Bank. (Source: @New_security8200/202089)
IDF reported more than 10 eliminations in Gaza during the week as part of ongoing counterterrorism operations. (Source: @New_security8200/202089)
IDF reported more than 100 arrests in the West Bank (Judea and Samaria) during the same period. (Source: @New_security8200/202089)

An alert was activated for suspected militant infiltration in the settlement of Beit Arieh in the Binyamin Brigade area following identification of two suspects in the settlement. (Source: @idf_telegram/24451)

Israel and Lebanon signed a US-brokered framework agreement requiring Hezbollah's disarmament and extension of Lebanese Armed Forces authority across all national territory. (Source: @inon_yttach/9176)

Popular protests erupted in Beirut around the government palace (Saray) and airport road following the agreement signing, with Lebanese Army deploying tear gas to disperse demonstrators. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80014)

Lebanese PM Salam affirmed Lebanon's primary obligation under the new framework is extending state authority through the Lebanese Armed Forces across all national territory. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4410)

Hezbollah members reportedly took to the streets of Beirut following the Lebanon-Israel agreement signing, raising concerns about potential internal Lebanese conflict. (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74401)
Israel and Lebanon agreed on two areas adjacent to the Yellow Line recommended by IDF for a pilot program for Hezbollah dismantlement. (Source: @inon_yttach/9176)

No specific operational developments regarding Syria or Iraq were reported in the provided messages beyond the West Bank arrests mentioned in the weekly IDF summary. (Source: @New_security8200/202089)
US CENTCOM struck Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar facilities on June 26 in response to Iran's attack on M/V Ever Lovely. (Source: @shin_persian/7223)
The UAE's NCEMA reported a technical glitch in the emergency alert system that caused incorrect alert messages to be sent on Friday evening, later resolved. (Source: @shin_persian/7214)
An alert was received in the UAE following detection of launches, which was later determined to be a launch toward a ship in the Strait before the incident concluded. (Source: @pkpoi/142987)

Iranian IRGC declared the southern corridor in the Strait of Hormuz near Omani waters as illegal and unacceptable, insisting only the route near Iranian shores is the legal passage. (Source: @VahidOnline/76691)

key_developments: US CENTCOM conducted strikes on June 26 against Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar facilities, responding to Iran's June 25 drone attack on Singapore-flagged M/V Ever Lovely in the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @shin_persian/7223)
Iran's IRGC naval forces attacked US force positions in retaliation for American strikes on southern Iran, according to Iranian state media reports. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373061)

Israel and Lebanon signed a US-brokered framework agreement requiring Hezbollah disarmament, with Israel maintaining its Yellow Line security zone until armed groups are dismantled. (Source: @inon_yttach/9176)

IDF Unit Egoz directed an airstrike eliminating seven Hezbollah militants at al-Manzala in southern Lebanon who were transferring weapons into a firing position against Israeli forces. (Source: @amitsegal/62556)

IRGC returned three foreign tankers from the southern corridor of the Strait of Hormuz, declaring the Oman-IMO proposed route illegal and asserting only the Iranian-designated passage is legitimate. (Source: @VahidOnline/76691)

Popular protests erupted in Beirut around the government palace and airport road after the Lebanon-Israel agreement, with Lebanese Army deploying tear gas against demonstrators. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80014)

Israeli Defense Minister Katz issued a Persian-language warning to Iran that any attack on Israel would be Tehran's greatest mistake, stating Israeli forces are prepared to complete their mission. (Source: @amitsegal/62555)
sections.threat_assessment: The situation is highly escalating across multiple fronts. The US-Iran direct military exchange in the Strait of Hormuz represents a significant escalation following Iran's drone attack on commercial shipping, with CENTCOM explicitly framing its strikes as enforcement of the ceasefire agreement. Iran's immediate retaliation against US force positions risks a broader cycle of direct military confrontation. Simultaneously, the Israel-Lebanon framework agreement has triggered domestic instability in Lebanon, with Hezbollah members reportedly mobilizing in Beirut streets, raising the risk of internal Lebanese conflict. Israel's rejection of over 10 Lebanese-American proposals and insistence on active Lebanese Army enforcement suggests implementation challenges ahead.
sections.regional_response: The UAE experienced a technical emergency alert malfunction unrelated to actual hostilities, indicating heightened regional alert postures. Lebanese popular sentiment turned sharply against the agreement, with protests at the government palace and airport road requiring Lebanese Army intervention with tear gas. Iran's IRGC escalated its posture in the Strait of Hormuz by physically intercepting tankers and declaring alternative transit routes illegal, asserting unilateral control over the waterway. Israel's Defense Minister Katz directly addressed Iran in Persian, signaling an intent to deter through direct communication. Lebanese PM Salam publicly committed to extending state authority through the LAF, aligning with the framework's core requirement.
sections.intelligence_notes: Several information gaps require monitoring: the exact locations of US strikes on Iran and IRGC counter-strikes are not specified beyond general references to southern Iran and coastal areas. The casualty figures for both the US strikes on Iran and IRGC attacks on US positions remain unreported. The specific terms of the Israel-Lebanon framework agreement beyond the Yellow Line security zone and Hezbollah disarmament requirements are not fully detailed in available sources. The status of the M/V Ever Lovely's damage and whether it continued its voyage is partially confirmed but operational details are limited. The scale and organization of Hezbollah's reported street presence in Beirut remains unclear from available reporting.
